messages.content: the 3 particles of the atom are:

1. Protons: These are positively charged particles found in the nucleus of an atom. They have a mass of approximately 1 atomic mass unit (amu) and a charge of +1.

2. Neutrons: These are neutral particles found in the nucleus of an atom. They have a mass of approximately 1 amu and no charge (neutral).

3. Electrons: These are negatively charged particles found in the electron cloud surrounding the nucleus of an atom. They have a very small mass (approximately 1/1836 amu) and a charge of -1.
their respective charges are:

The charges of the three particles are:

1. Protons: +1 charge.
2. Neutrons: No charge (neutral).
3. Electrons: -1 charge.
the number of protons in one atom of an element determines the atom's _____, and the number of electrons determines the ____ of the element.
The number of protons in one atom of an element determines the atom's atomic number, and the number of electrons determines the element's overall charge or its neutral state.
fill the blanks
The number of protons in one atom of an element determines the atom's atomic number, and the number of electrons determines the element's overall charge or its stability.
don't change the text just fill up the blanks
The number of protons in one atom of an element determines the atom's atomic number, and the number of electrons determines the element's overall charge or its neutral state.
the number of protons in one atom of an element determines the atom’s _____, and the number of electrons determines the ____ of the element. (fill the blaks and don't change the text)
The number of protons in one atom of an element determines the atom's atomic number, and the number of electrons determines the charge of the element.

The atomic number tells you the number of _____ in one atom of an element. It also tells you the number of _____ in a neutral atom of that element. The atomic number gives the "identity" of an element as well as its location on the periodic table. No two different elements will have the ____ atomic number. (fill the blanks and don't change the text)

The atomic number tells you the number of protons in one atom of an element. It also tells you the number of electrons in a neutral atom of that element. The atomic number gives the "identity" of an element as well as its location on the periodic table. No two different elements will have the same atomic number.
The ____ of an element is the average mass of an element's naturally occurring atom, or isotopes, taking into account the ___ of each isotope. (fill the blanks and don't change the text)
The atomic mass of an element is the average mass of an element's naturally occurring atom, or isotopes, taking into account the abundance of each isotope.
The ____ of an element is the total number of protons and neutrons in the ____ of the atom.
The mass number of an element is the total number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus of the atom.
The ____ of an element is the total number of protons and neutrons in the ____ of the atom. (fill the blanks and don’t change the text)
The mass number of an element is the total number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus of the atom.

The mass number is used to calculate the number of ___ in one atom of an element. In order to calculate the number of neutrons you must subtract the ___ from the ____. (fill the blanks and don’t change the text)
The mass number is used to calculate the number of neutrons in one atom of an element. In order to calculate the number of neutrons, you must subtract the atomic number from the mass number.
